name: SC Behavioral Rubric
description: Slack Classifier evaluation rubric — noise filtering, Company resolution from unstructured text, thread context usage, classification accuracy

rules.name: Bot and Noise Message Filtering
rules.description: Bot messages, join/leave notifications, integration alerts, and very short reactions (ok, thanks, emoji-only) must be classified as irrelevant. These should be filtered early before Company resolution is attempted.

pass_conditions: Very short non-substantive messages (emoji, ok, thanks) transitioned to irrelevant
Automated report outputs transitioned to irrelevant even when they mention customer names
No Company resolution attempted for noise messages
fail_conditions: Automated report output classified as customer-relevant despite being bot-generated
Company entity_resolve called for a message that is clearly noise

examples.violation: SC classified a #test-reports automated weekly report as customer-relevant because it mentioned 'Insurica' in the report title
examples.correct: SC identified the message as a bot-generated report in #test-reports and transitioned to irrelevant without attempting Company resolution

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If the message was clearly bot-generated (format, channel, sender) but not filtered: missing_rule. If the message was ambiguous between bot and human content: skill_gap.

rules.name: Thread Context Loaded for Threaded Messages
rules.description: When a message has thread_ts set (it is part of a thread), SC must load the thread via slackmessage list before classifying. Short replies like 'on it' or 'updating now' are only classifiable with the parent message that names the customer.

pass_conditions: When thread_ts is present, agent called slackmessage list with the thread_ts to load thread context
Classification decision incorporated thread context, not just the individual message
Standalone messages (no thread_ts) were classified without attempting thread load
fail_conditions: Agent classified a threaded reply without loading thread context
Agent marked a threaded reply as irrelevant when the parent message clearly referenced a customer
Agent called slackmessage list for a standalone message (wasted call)

examples.violation: SC received message 'on it, will update the config now' with thread_ts set, classified as irrelevant without loading the parent message that said 'INSURICA needs their CTA button updated'
examples.correct: SC loaded thread context via slackmessage list, found parent message referencing INSURICA, classified the reply as customer-relevant with company=INSURICA

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If thread context was never loaded despite thread_ts being present: missing_rule. If thread was loaded but customer reference in parent was missed: associate_error.

rules.name: Company Resolution Uses entity_resolve
rules.description: When SC identifies a customer name in the message text, it must call company entity_resolve to link to an existing Company. SC must never auto-create Companies from Slack — Slack mentions are noisy and Company creation should come from stronger signals (email, meetings).

pass_conditions: Agent called company entity_resolve with the identified customer name
If resolve returned 1 candidate with score 1.0, agent linked to that Company
If resolve returned 0 candidates, agent classified with company=null (no auto-create)
No company create calls were made
fail_conditions: Agent created a new Company from a Slack message mention
Agent identified a customer name but did not call company entity_resolve
Agent set company to a string name instead of a resolved entity ID

examples.violation: SC identified 'Alliance' in a Slack message and created a new Company entity instead of resolving to the existing Alliance Insurance
examples.correct: SC identified 'Alliance' in the message, called company entity_resolve with name 'Alliance', got 1 candidate (Alliance Insurance), linked the message to that Company ID

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If company create was called: missing_rule (hard rule violation). If entity_resolve was not called: missing_rule. If resolve returned 0 for a known company name (name mismatch): context_gap.

rules.name: Multi-Customer Messages Link Primary and Flag Secondary
rules.description: When a message mentions multiple customers (e.g., a status update covering several accounts), SC must link to the first/primary customer mentioned and create a ReviewItem noting the additional customers.

pass_conditions: Company field set to the primary (first) customer mentioned
ReviewItem created noting the additional customer references
Message classified as customer-relevant (not irrelevant despite multi-customer ambiguity)
fail_conditions: Agent only linked one customer and silently dropped the others without a ReviewItem
Agent classified as irrelevant because of multi-customer ambiguity
Agent attempted to link multiple companies to a single SlackMessage entity

examples.violation: SC received a status update mentioning Union General and Johnson Insurance, linked to Union General but did not create a ReviewItem for Johnson Insurance
examples.correct: SC linked to Union General (first mentioned), created ReviewItem noting Johnson Insurance also mentioned in the same update

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If ReviewItem was not created: missing_rule. If agent attempted multi-company linking: associate_error (not supported yet). If the secondary mention was too ambiguous to identify: skill_gap.

rules.name: Slack Scope Is Always Internal
rules.description: All Slack messages are internal scope because only team members are in the workspace. SC must never set scope to external.

pass_conditions: Scope field set to 'internal' on every classified SlackMessage
No scope determination logic attempted beyond setting internal
fail_conditions: Scope set to 'external' on any SlackMessage
Scope field missing from the update call

examples.violation: SC set scope=external on a message in #customer-implementation because it discussed a customer
examples.correct: SC set scope=internal on a customer-related message in #customer-implementation — discussing customers does not make the Slack message external

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If scope was set to external: associate_error. If scope field was missing: associate_error.

severity_definitions.high: Failures create wrong Company links, miss customer-relevant messages, or create entities that should not exist — directly causes data quality issues
severity_definitions.medium: Failures produce incomplete classifications or miss secondary context — degrades quality but doesn't corrupt the entity graph
severity_definitions.low: Failures affect audit trail or metadata completeness — no impact on entity graph or downstream processing
